name: notebook-session-labs
description: Connect AI assistants (Claude, GPT, etc.) to your Jupyter notebooks in VS Code. Let AI read, edit, and execute your notebook cells in real time through a secure local bridge.

commands.title: Notebook Session Labs: Show Bridge Status

notebookSessionLabs.bridge.enabled.description: Enable the local notebook bridge server

notebookSessionLabs.bridge.host.description: Host address for the bridge server (loopback only recommended)

notebookSessionLabs.bridge.port.description: Port for the bridge server (0 = ephemeral)

notebookSessionLabs.bridge.autoStart.description: Automatically start the bridge when a notebook is opened

enumDescriptions: Legacy — silently upgraded to 'token' for security. Token auth is always enforced.
Require an ephemeral bearer token for all bridge requests (always active)
notebookSessionLabs.bridge.authMode.description: Bridge authentication mode. Token auth is always enforced regardless of this setting. The token is auto-generated and written to the port file for MCP client discovery.

notebookSessionLabs.bridge.stateDir.description: Directory for bridge port files (used by Docker containers to discover the bridge). Leave empty for platform default: Linux/macOS/WSL → /tmp/notebook-session-labs, Windows → C:\Users\<you>\AppData\Local\Temp\notebook-session-labs. The actual resolved path is shown in the bridge status notification on startup. Mount this path to /tmp/notebook-session-labs in your Docker config.

notebookSessionLabs.output.maxSize.description: Maximum output size in bytes per cell

notebookSessionLabs.output.includeImages.description: Include image outputs in cell output data
